extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb claimed his third stage win in Paris sprint, threading a right-side gap in final 100m while GROENEWEGEN Dylan drifted left.
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE Julian repaying domestique debt to VIVIANI Elia by leading out sprint instead of targeting stage himself.
EWAN positioned deliberately far back with 100m remaining, exploiting chaos when GROENEWEGEN went left to find cleaner right-side lane.
Quick-Step train with MØRKØV Michael and RICHEZE Maximiliano failed when VIVIANI lost critical wheel through Concorde corner.
BOASSON HAGEN Edvald launched sprint earliest but faded fifth, revealing Dimension Data's desperation after 21 winless stages.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: Three stage wins in debut Tour demonstrates elite sprint finishing speed and tactical patience from deep positions.
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: Lost wheel exiting Concorde corner despite perfect leadout from two world champions, failed sprint execution.
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: neutral: Drift left in final meters cost stage win, suggesting anticipation failure under pressure despite strong speed.
rider_signals.BOASSON HAGEN Edvald: negative: Fifth after early launch reveals declining punch after 21 stages of failed breakaway attempts for Dimension Data.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Chose leadout duty over personal glory on final stage, signals team-first mentality despite two-week yellow jersey.
